commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Dream State graceful DEADLINE_EXCEEDED handling + bump event_timeout

kumiho-memory 0.3.14:
- DEADLINE_EXCEEDED from event stream is now logged at debug level
  (expected termination after draining events) instead of warning
- Default event_timeout bumped from 5.0s to 10.0s to accommodate
  network latency + auth overhead during event replay

commit.message:
Add HHI-Assist quantitative results table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add API retry logic and task kill switch

Retry logic (planner + executor):
- 3 attempts with 2/4/8s exponential backoff on API stream failures
- Yields status messages to UI during retries so user sees what's happening
- Gracefully falls back after exhausting retries instead of crashing

Kill switch:
- POST /api/kill/<task_id> endpoint sets a threading.Event
- Cancel event propagated: app.py → Orchestrator → planner/executor
- Checked at every stream chunk, before each tool call, between steps
- Red pulsing KILL button appears in UI when task is running
- Button sends kill signal, task winds down cleanly with "cancelled" status
- New "cancelled" message type styled in UI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(cache): replace ambiguities with unified annotations system

Consolidate the separate ambiguities field into a general-purpose
annotations system with 4 types: domain, architecture, usage, ambiguity.
This enables AI to proactively record business context and design
rationale during indexing, not just questions.

- CacheEntry: ambiguities -> annotations [{type, content, added_by, added_at}]
- New MCP tools: get_annotations (filtered/paginated), add_annotation
- resolve_ambiguity now operates on ambiguity-type annotations
- Index skill generates all 4 annotation types during analysis

Improve mobile touch UX and fix zoom border scaling

- Skip insets on narrow viewports (width < 700) so touchAction: none
  doesn't block scrolling to reach off-screen inset panels
- Enable touch hover: remove pointerType === 'touch' guard on
  pointerenter, add clearPreviousHover() to restore previous region
  when tapping a new one; keep touch guard on pointerleave so
  highlight persists until next tap
- Add non-scaling-stroke to main map paths, border mesh, context
  layer, and country outline so borders stay thin when pinch-zooming

puzzle_search.c:

commit.message:
Fix plugin loading: use --developer-mode --classpath instead of sideloaded-plugins

RuneLite doesn't have a sideloaded-plugins directory. External plugins
need to be loaded via --developer-mode with --classpath pointing to the
JAR. The installer now creates a desktop shortcut that launches RuneLite
with the correct flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add The Corn Maze - a first-person corn maze simulator game

A relaxing, artistic first-person game featuring:
- 5 levels (5x5 to 22x22 procedural mazes)
- Raycasting 3D rendering with corn stalk walls, dirt floor, golden sky
- Atmospheric effects: dust motes, corn swaying, vignette
- Character comments when hitting dead ends (16 variations)
- Fog-of-war minimap and compass
- Increasingly absurd celebration sequences per level:
  * Level 1: Farmer clapping
  * Level 2: Small-town crowd with banner
  * Level 3: Fireworks and champagne
  * Level 4: Elephants, bald eagles, moonwalking T-Rex
  * Level 5: UFO, whale with trumpet, sentient dancing corn, reality breaks
- Particle effects for confetti and fireworks
- Complete end-game screen with credits

Controls: Arrow keys to move/look, Enter to start/next level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: add per-user rate limiting for Discord message injection (WOP-1723) (#84)

* security: add per-user rate limiting for Discord message injection (WOP-1723)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #84 review issues (WOP-1723)

- rate-limiter: extract pruneExpired helper shared by isRateLimited and
  getRemainingRequests, fixing stale-entry inconsistency (sourcery)
- rate-limiter: delete map entry when timestamps array empties, preventing
  unbounded Map growth in busy guilds (greptile)
- rate-limiter: remove logger.warn from isRateLimited — caller in
  handleMessage already logs with richer context, eliminating double-warn
  per rate-limited event (greptile/sourcery)
- event-handlers: promote inline import("./rate-limiter.js").RateLimiter
  to a top-level import type (greptile)
- event-handlers: move rate limit check to before attachment download so
  rate-limited users cannot trigger CDN fetches/disk writes (greptile critical)
- event-handlers: send rate limit notice via DM instead of channel reply
  to avoid channel noise from spamming users (greptile/coderabbit)
- tests: add author.send mock, update assertion to check DM not reply,
  add new test verifying saveAttachments is not called for rate-limited users

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Pattern Federation — FSM Visualization & Real-time Updates (#81)

* feat(intelligence): add behavioral layer for Protocol Federation

Add the 7th intelligence layer (behavioral) that renders Protocol FSMs
in the graph visualization with Protocol/ProtocolState nodes and
HAS_STATE/TRANSITION/BELONGS_TO_SKILL edges.

- Add IntelligenceLayer 'behavioral', entity types, relation types
- Add ProtocolNodeData, ProtocolStateNodeData interfaces
- Add BehavioralLayerSummary to IntelligenceSummary
- Add behavioral layer config (orange-500), edge styles, node sizes
- Add 'Behavioral' visibility preset, update 'Full' preset

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(intelligence): add ProtocolNode, ProtocolStateNode & ProtocolContextCard

Create the frontend components for Protocol Federation visualization:
- ProtocolNode.tsx: FSM node with category colors (system=blue, business=orange)
- ProtocolStateNode.tsx: state circle with state_type icons (start/intermediate/terminal)
- ProtocolContextCard.tsx: inspector card with states, transitions, skill link
- Register both nodes in intelligenceNodeTypes registry
- Wire ProtocolContextCard into NodeInspector
- Add getProtocol() to intelligenceApi service
- Add behavioral layer to useIntelligenceGraph fetch + mapLayer
- Add ProtocolDetailApi, ProtocolStateApi, ProtocolTransitionApi types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(plan): add Wave View with DAG/Waves toggle in plan detail

New WaveView component displays execution waves as horizontal columns
with task cards, conflict highlighting (orange ring + warning icon),
and a summary bar showing wave count, max parallelism, and critical
path length. Integrated into PlanDetailPage with a DAG/Waves toggle
that computes waves on demand via the get_waves API endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(intelligence): add real-time FSM viewer & protocol run visualization

- ProtocolRunViewer: SVG-based FSM viewer with BFS layout, pulsing
  current state, animated transitions, and progress bar overlay
- ProtocolContextCard: show active run with compact FSM viewer inline
- ProtocolNode: animated overlay (pulsing ring, status dot) driven by
  runStatus from WebSocket events
- LearningTimeline & ActivityHeatmap3D: protocol_transition events
- useProtocolRunEvents: hook bridging CRUD EventBus → intelligence graph
  atoms for live runStatus updates on protocol nodes
- ProtocolRunViz: chat VizBlock component for inline FSM run display
- Added protocol_run entity type and progress action to event types
- Added listProtocols, listRuns, getRun to intelligence service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(lint): resolve React 19 lint errors in ProtocolRunViewer and useProtocolRunEvents

- ProtocolRunViewer: replace Date.now() in render with useState+useEffect
  interval pattern for live elapsed time display
- useProtocolRunEvents: move ref assignment into useEffect to avoid
  writing refs during render, copy ref in cleanup to avoid stale access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(intelligence): address PR review critical issues

- Fix BFS infinite loop on cyclic FSMs in ProtocolRunViewer (use visited set)
- Fix setTimeout leak in useProtocolRunEvents (track + cleanup timers on unmount)
- Cancel stale timers when new events arrive for same protocol

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(intelligence): add ContextRadar, ProtocolRanking & routing visualization

Extract shared RadarChart SVG component from ContextRadarViz into ui/.
Add ContextRadar for protocol affinity dimension display, ProtocolRanking
for ranked protocol list with expandable dimension bars. Integrate
"Why Activated" panel in ProtocolContextCard with radar chart, color-coded
dimension bars, and explanation text. Add routing API types and service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(composer): add Pattern Composer UI with NotePool + FSMCanvas

- NotePool: draggable note list with type/search filters (@dnd-kit)
- FSMCanvas: interactive FSM editor with @xyflow/react
  - Double-click to add states (start/intermediate/terminal)
  - Drag handles to create transitions with trigger/guard dialog
  - Drop notes from pool onto states (note binding)
- PatternComposer: 3-panel layout (NotePool | Canvas | Properties)
  - Properties panel: name, description, category, stats, simulate
  - Compose button → POST /api/protocols/compose
- Types: ComposeProtocolRequest, SimulateRequest/Response
- API: intelligenceApi.composeProtocol + simulateProtocol

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(composer): add TriggerBuilder, simulate preview, and export JSON

TriggerBuilder provides 5-dimension slider UI for RelevanceVector
(phase, structure, domain, resource, lifecycle) with color-coded
semantic labels and reset functionality.

PatternComposer PropertiesPanel now integrates:
- TriggerBuilder for relevance vector editing
- Simulate activation button (real API call via simulateProtocol)
- ContextRadar visualization of simulation results
- Export JSON download (.compose.json blob)
- Created protocol/skill ID display after successful compose

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(frontend): add Skill Registry browser, TrustBadge and ImportWizard

- Add registry types (PublishedSkillSummary, TrustLevel, TrustComponents)
- Add PortableProtocol type and protocols field to SkillPackage
- Create registryApi service (search, get, publish, import)
- Create TrustBadge component (compact badge + detailed score bar)
- Create SkillBrowser with search, trust filtering, and pagination
- Create ImportWizard dialog (preview, import, success/error steps)
- Integrate "Browse Registry" tab in SkillsPage alongside "My Skills"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): add MilestoneStatusBadge and replace inline status styling

Add MilestoneStatusBadge component to Badge.tsx following the existing
pattern (PlanStatusBadge, TaskStatusBadge, etc.). Replace all inline
milestone status color logic in ExpandableMilestoneRow and
WorkspaceDetailPage with the shared badge component.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(intelligence): enhance graph with entity legend, activation dimming, new node types and 3D improvements

Major intelligence graph visualization improvements:

- Add entity legend (bottom-left) showing active entity types with colored dots
- Enhance edge highlighting: hover (amber) + selection (cyan) coexist, edges dim
  during spreading activation for non-activated subgraph
- Add ⌘K button as prominent CTA for Spreading Activation search
- Node inspector now adapts width in fullscreen mode, collapse tab sticks to panel edge
- Add EnumNode, TraitNode, FeatureGraphNode custom node components
- 3D view: add protocol/skill/feature_graph node objects, improve layout
- IntelligencePage: add behavioral layer card (protocols, states, transitions)
- Update types and constants for new entity types and edge styles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(intelligence): optimize graph loading — fetch visible layers only, add edge budget

Major performance improvements for the Intelligence Graph page:

1. **Fetch only visible layers**: instead of loading all 6 layers upfront,
   only request the currently enabled ones (default: code, knowledge, fabric).
   Reduces initial data volume by ~60%.

2. **Limit parameter**: pass limit=1000 (configurable via graphNodeLimitAtom)
   to the graph API instead of relying on the backend default of 5000.

3. **Incremental layer loading**: switching presets only fetches layers not
   yet loaded (debounced 300ms). Already-loaded layers are preserved.

4. **Edge budget culling**: new budgetedEdgesAtom sorts edges by
   EDGE_RENDER_PRIORITY (AFFECTS > IMPORTS > EXTENDS > ...) and caps
   at MAX_VISIBLE_EDGES=2000. Shows "N edges hidden" badge when active.

5. **Remove double dagre layout**: fetchGraph no longer calls layoutGraph()
   — only the useMemo on visible nodes performs layout (was wasted work).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(intelligence): offload dagre layout to Web Worker

Move the expensive O(V+E) dagre graph layout computation off the main
thread into a dedicated Web Worker. This keeps the UI responsive while
computing positions for large graphs (1000+ nodes).

- Add src/workers/dagreWorker.ts with serializable message protocol
- Replace synchronous useMemo layout with async useEffect + worker
- Add layoutVersionRef to cancel stale layout results
- Show "Computing layout…" spinner during worker computation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(intelligence): CSS-driven edge highlighting, remove O(n) hover useMemo

Replace the highlightedEdges useMemo that recreated all 2000+ edge objects
on every hover event with a zero-JS-overhead approach:

- Default edges: dynamic <style> tag with CSS attribute selectors on
  data-testid to dim/highlight edges (edge IDs use | separator for
  reliable substring matching)
- Custom edges (synapse, co_changed, affects): read hoveredNodeIdAtom
  and selectedNodeIdAtom directly from Jotai atoms instead of receiving
  _highlighted/_hasHover data flags
- Spreading activation: kept as JS-only path (rare event, can't express
  Set membership in CSS)

Result: hover on a node with 2000 edges now triggers zero JS edge object
creation — only a CSS style recalculation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(intelligence): progressive layer loading with per-layer spinners

Fetch primary layers (code+fabric) first for fast first paint, then
load remaining layers incrementally. Add per-layer loading indicators
in LayerControls and debounced incremental fetch on layer toggle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(intelligence): 3D default views, canvas resize fix, InstancedMesh optimization

- Set 3D as default view mode for both Intelligence Graph and Vector Space
- Add AutoFitView component with ResizeObserver to fix ReactFlow canvas
  not resizing after navigation (sidebar toggle, project switch)
- Wrap ReactFlow in ReactFlowProvider for programmatic fitView access
- Refactor VectorSpace3D to use THREE.InstancedMesh instead of individual
  meshes per point: single draw call for all points, single draw call for
  glow halos. Raycaster uses instanceId for O(1) hit detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(intelligence): overlay states, fullscreen sizing, VectorSpace 3D fallback

- Use allNodes (raw API data) for overlay state checks instead of
  layouted nodes — fixes "No intelligence data" flash in 3D mode where
  dagre worker doesn't run
- Remove negative margins in fullscreen mode and set explicit 100vw
  width — fixes canvas stuck in top-right corner at partial width
- Add 3D→2D fallback in VectorSpace: if 3D projection returns 0 points,
  automatically retry with 2D dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(intelligence): remove w-full so negative margins properly expand canvas width

Block elements with width:auto + negative margins expand to fill beyond
their parent. Explicit w-full (width:100%) prevents this, capping the
element at parent content width. Remove w-full when negative margins are
active; keep it only for embedded/fullscreen modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(graph3d): use absolute positioning for full-width 3D canvas

The 3D graph container used `w-full h-full` (width: 100%) which resolves
against the parent's content box. When the parent expands via negative
margins + auto width for full-bleed, the percentage-based child width
didn't capture the expanded size. Switching to `absolute inset-0` makes
the container fill the nearest positioned ancestor (the outer relative
div) directly, correctly inheriting the full expanded dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(graph3d): always render container div so ResizeObserver fires

The 3D graph was stuck at default 800×600 (appearing as 1600×1200 on
Retina) because the component returned null when graphData was empty.
This caused the useEffect([]) to find containerRef=null on mount, so
the ResizeObserver was never attached. When data arrived and the div
appeared, the effect didn't re-run.

Fix: always render the container div with absolute inset-0, and
conditionally render ForceGraph3D inside it. The ResizeObserver now
correctly measures the container on mount and passes accurate
dimensions to the 3D engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Restrict runs to authenticated users and add rate limiting (#15)

* Restrict runs to authenticated users and add rate limiting

- GET /api/runs now filters by user_id and returns empty list for unauthenticated users
- POST /api/runs/start requires authentication and enforces 10-run-per-day limit per user
- POST /api/runs/:runId/stop and DELETE require user ownership verification
- Added verifyRunOwnership helper for consistency across protected endpoints
- Frontend RunsPage shows login prompt when not authenticated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reload runs when auth state changes after in-app sign-in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle stop during run initialization race window

Store userId in in-memory RunState so ownership can be verified even
before the DB row exists. Reorder stop endpoint to check isRunActive
first, then verify ownership from in-memory state for initializing runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ownership check on fork source, error handling, and delete UX

- verifyRunOwnership now returns 500 on DB errors instead of silent 404
- Fork (sourceRunId) now verifies the user owns the source run
- Skip unnecessary getRuns() API call when user is not authenticated
- Fix delete confirmation: confirm() returns boolean not null, so
  Cancel was deleting without cleanup instead of cancelling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: support auth-disabled deployments and legacy null user_id runs

- GET /api/runs returns own runs + legacy null user_id runs for
  authenticated users, all runs when auth is disabled
- Start endpoint skips rate limit and auth requirement when no token
  is provided (auth-disabled mode)
- verifyRunOwnership allows null user_id runs to be managed by anyone
- Frontend checks authEnabled before showing sign-in gate, loads runs
  normally when auth is not configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: close auth bypass and restrict legacy null user_id runs

Server always has Supabase configured, so missing token means
unauthenticated — not auth-disabled. This fixes two issues:

- GET /api/runs returns empty list for unauthenticated callers
  instead of all runs; POST /api/runs/start requires auth and
  always enforces rate limit; fork ownership always checked
- Legacy runs (null user_id) remain visible in the runs list but
  cannot be stopped, deleted, or forked since null !== user.id

Removes the auth-disabled frontend bypass since the server always
requires auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: graceful shutdown for active benchmark runs

On SIGTERM/SIGINT, signal all active runs to stop via requestStop(),
then wait for them to drain from the activeRuns map (up to 30s timeout).
The existing shouldStop mechanism causes phases to finish their current
work and checkpoint before exiting. A final flush() ensures all pending
checkpoint writes complete before the process exits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-resume interrupted runs after graceful shutdown

Add "interrupted" RunStatus for runs stopped by server shutdown (as
opposed to user-initiated stops which set "failed"). On startup,
resumeInterruptedRuns() queries for interrupted runs and restarts
them using checkpoint data. If auto-resume fails for a run, it falls
back to "failed" status so it doesn't retry indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent clobbering completed runs and add interrupted status to DB

- Only mark runs still in activeRuns map as interrupted, skipping
  runs that completed during the drain window
- Add migration 008 to allow 'interrupted' in the runs.status check
  constraint, which previously only permitted initializing/running/
  completed/failed
- Add error handling for the shutdown DB update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guard interrupted update against late-completing runs in SQL

Add .neq('status', 'completed') to the shutdown update so runs that
finish between the in-memory check and the DB write aren't clobbered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Sim All redirecting to free agency instead of draft recap

Root cause: simToEndDraft() unconditionally called advanceToFreeAgency(),
which changed the phase to freeAgency. The draft page's useEffect then
auto-redirected to /free-agency before our navigation could take effect.

Fix: Add { skipAdvance: true } option to simToEndDraft() so Sim All
callers can prevent the auto-advance. The draft-recap page now has an
"Advance to Free Agency" button so the user can proceed when ready.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: inject live injury cache into AI chat for player/team queries

Add _build_injury_context() to handle_message():
- Detects sport (NBA/NFL/NHL) from keywords in the user message
- Detects specific player name mentions (star players from _STAR_MULTIPLIERS)
- Pulls from the verified injury cache (ESPN + Sleeper + NBA PDF)
- Injects formatted injury data directly into the AI prompt
- Shows source confidence badges: (official), (confirmed), (⚠️ conflicting)
- Falls back to top-10 severity list if no specific team/player match

Before: "Is LeBron injured?" → AI guesses from stale training data
After:  "Is LeBron injured?" → AI answers from verified live cache

Hard cap at 15 records per query to keep prompt size reasonable.
Cache source line tells AI where data came from and when refreshed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
